summary: Russia struck the UN World Food Programme depot in Dnipro with an Iskander missile, destroying food supplies for 130,000 people. Ukrainian officials report 70‑100 civilian deaths weekly from Russian attacks, while Shahed‑136 UAV threats increase from the Gvardiyiskyi area. Multiple UAV sightings are reported crossing from Russian‑held Belgorod into northern and eastern Ukrainian regions.
key_points: Russia used an Iskander missile to hit the UN World Food Programme depot in Dnipro, destroying food for 130,000 people valued at $1.4 million (Source: @UkraineNow/81473).
Ukrainian Interior Minister Igor Klymenko says 70‑100 civilians are killed each week by Russian drone, missile and bomb attacks (Source: @UkraineNow/81472).
Shahed‑136 strike UAVs have been launched from the Gvardiyiskyi area, raising threat levels (Source: @eRadarrua/93817).
UAVs from Belgorod region are reported heading south into Sumy and north into Kharkiv oblasts (Source: @kpszsu/62941).
UAVs observed moving eastward in Sumy region toward Hotin and Boromlya (Source: @kpszsu/62945).
UAVs detected heading northward into Kharkiv from Belgorod (Source: @kpszsu/62946).
UAV activity noted near Chernihiv, north of the city in the Sedneve area (Source: @eRadarrua/93843).

sections.executive_summary: Russia’s Iskander strike on the UN food depot in Dnipro eliminated supplies for 130,000 civilians. Ukrainian officials report 70‑100 civilian deaths weekly from Russian attacks, while Shahed‑136 UAV threats rise from the Gvardiyiskyi area. Numerous UAV incursions from Belgorod into Sumy, Kharkiv and Chernihiv regions increase pressure on Ukrainian air defenses.

points: The Ukrainian Interior Ministry states that 70‑100 civilians are killed each week by Russian drone, ballistic, cruise missile and aerial bomb attacks, highlighting a high civilian toll (Source: @UkraineNow/81472).

Ukrainian authorities report a targeted Iskander missile strike on the UN World Food Programme depot in Dnipro, destroying food stocks intended for 130,000 people, valued at $1.4 million (Source: @UkraineNow/81473).
Ukrainian forces are reportedly collecting donations to fund FPV drones for reconnaissance, indicating a push to enhance local UAV capabilities (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67234).
Ukrainian officials note increased threat levels of Shahed‑136 strike UAVs launched from the Gvardiyiskyi area, prompting heightened air defense alerts (Source: @eRadarrua/93817).
Russian media TASS reports the development of a charging armored evacuation vehicle for MLRS systems, indicating ongoing equipment modernization (Source: @tass_agency/377691).
Reports from TASS indicate Russian mobilization orders are being issued, calling reservists to military commissariats and offering contracts with the Russian army (Source: @UkraineNow/81466).
Russian authorities announced a systematic bombing campaign against Kiev, with reported shortages of air‑defence missiles on the Russian side (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30430).
TASS noted a fire and explosion in a private house in Lipetsk, with casualties, reflecting domestic incidents within Russia (Source: @tass_agency/377660).
UAVs from Belgorod region are observed heading south into Sumy oblast, targeting areas near the city of Sumy (Source: @kpszsu/62941).
UAVs on the Sumy front are reported moving toward Hotin and Boromlya, indicating possible reconnaissance or strike missions (Source: @kpszsu/62945).
UAV activity in Dnipropetrovsk region is noted, with drones heading southeast toward the settlement of Pysmenne (Source: @kpszsu/62931).
Ukrainian reports mention Shahed attacks near Kyiv/Brovary, suggesting eastern approaches to the capital are under UAV threat (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67229).
UAVs from Belgorod are observed moving northward into Kharkiv oblast, specifically toward Kharkiv/Rogany, indicating pressure on the northern front (Source: @kpszsu/62920).
UAVs from Belgorod are also reported heading north into northern Kharkiv region (Source: @kpszsu/62946).
A UAV was detected north of Chernihiv in the Sedneve area, showing activity on the northern front (Source: @eRadarrua/93843).
Shahed‑136 UAV threats are highlighted from the Gvardiyiskyi area, which lies on the northern approach to Ukrainian territory (Source: @eRadarrua/93817).
No explicit southern front operational reports were found in the provided messages.
The Iskander strike on the WFP depot in Dnipro, while central, affects logistics for southern supply lines (Source: @UkraineNow/81473).
Ukrainian mobilization calls may impact reserve forces in southern regions (Source: @UkraineNow/81466).
No further southern front UAV or artillery activity was detailed in the source set.
Shahed‑136 strike UAVs have been launched from the Gvardiyiskyi area, raising the aerial threat level (Source: @eRadarrua/93817).

Multiple UAV incursions from Russian‑held Belgorod into Sumy, Kharkiv and Chernihiv regions suggest intensified air‑space violations (Sources: @kpszsu/62941, @kpszsu/62945, @kpszsu/62946, @eRadarrua/93843).
Ukrainian officials report a shortage of air‑defence missiles, with requests for 5‑10 missiles to cover Kiev (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30430).
Ukrainian civilian casualties from Russian air attacks remain high, with 70‑100 deaths per week (Source: @UkraineNow/81472).

key_developments: Russia employed an Iskander missile to strike the UN World Food Programme depot in Dnipro, destroying food supplies for 130,000 people valued at $1.4 million (Source: @UkraineNow/81473).
Ukrainian Interior Minister Igor Klymenko reports 70‑100 civilian deaths each week from Russian drone, missile and bomb attacks (Source: @UkraineNow/81472).
Shahed‑136 strike UAVs have been launched from the Gvardiyiskyi area, increasing the aerial threat to Ukrainian defenses (Source: @eRadarrua/93817).
UAVs from Russia’s Belgorod region are observed heading south into Sumy oblast and north into Kharkiv oblast, indicating cross‑border drone activity (Sources: @kpszsu/62941, @kpszsu/62945).
UAVs detected north of Chernihiv in the Sedneve area show expanding UAV incursions on the northern front (Source: @eRadarrua/93843).
Russian mobilization orders are being issued, calling reservists to military commissariats and offering contracts with the Russian army (Source: @UkraineNow/81466).
Russian officials claim a shortage of air‑defence missiles, while Ukraine requests additional missiles to protect Kiev (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30430).
sections.threat_assessment: Ukrainian territory faces a multi‑vector aerial threat, with Shahed‑136 strike UAVs and numerous cross‑border drones from Belgorod targeting northern and eastern regions. The Iskander missile strike on critical humanitarian infrastructure in Dnipro demonstrates Russia's willingness to use high‑precision weapons against logistics. Civilian casualty rates remain high, underscoring the intensity of Russian air and missile attacks and the strain on Ukraine's air‑defence capabilities.
sections.regional_response: Ukraine is intensifying air‑defence alerts and seeking additional missile supplies to counter the growing UAV threat, while also mobilizing domestic fundraising for FPV drones. International partners have condemned attacks on civilian infrastructure, but concrete support for additional air‑defence assets remains limited. Russian mobilization efforts aim to replenish forces, potentially increasing the scale of future operations.
sections.intelligence_notes: Monitoring of UAV flight paths from Belgorod into Sumy and Kharkiv should be prioritized to anticipate strike vectors. The Gvardiyiskyi launch site for Shahed‑136 UAVs requires surveillance for possible interdiction. Continued assessment of civilian casualty reports will help gauge the effectiveness of Russian air campaigns and inform humanitarian response planning.
